queries.query: our computer-use agent can't tell what state our app is in — the status is just a colored badge. make the state machine-readable

should our agent be allowed to fire the 'delete account' button on its own, or does that need confirmation? design the approval surface
our UI is great for humans but an agent acting on it can't see the rules buried in tooltips — resolve this human-vs-agent conflict
make our web app agent-operable so a computer-use agent can perceive and act on it safely — full review and score
an agent retried our checkout and double-charged the customer — make the action idempotent and retry-safe
when an agent acts on a user's account, how do we show it's acting on their behalf with scoped, revocable consent?
our action results only show as a toast that vanishes — the agent can't tell if the save worked. review the state surface
redesign our marketing homepage to convert better for human visitors
run a WCAG accessibility audit of our product for human users with disabilities
curate our llms.txt and AGENTS.md so an agent can read our docs
design the typed tool schema and token-exchange for our Agent SDK
set up a trace-and-eval loop to watch our agent's quality in production
review our human checkout flow for friction and drop-off
